rationale: The cited paper appears as one example in a grouped citation supporting a broad claim that expectation-confirmation effects on satisfaction have been shown in prior marketing research. In the citing paper, it is used only as background evidence alongside several other sources, without any discussion of its specific assortment-size mechanism, design, or findings. There is no clear sign that the citing study builds directly on, refines, applies, or challenges this particular paper beyond that general literature mention.
